narrative.context: Tomorrow is your project viva. You feel nervous, and your mind keeps replaying what could go wrong.
narrative.prompt: How do you use your emotional state to prepare effectively tonight?

options.label: Channel the alertness into a plan
options.description: Name the nervous energy as a signal. Create a short checklist (key slides, likely questions, 30-minute review blocks).

ei.rationale: Uses emotion as information to prioritize attention and problem-solve, improving thinking without suppressing feelings.

options.label: Distract to calm down, then study
options.description: Watch something light for an hour to reduce stress, then attempt revision.

ei.rationale: Regulates arousal indirectly; can help thinking if time is controlled, but risks avoidance and reduced preparation.

options.label: Force yourself to study harder with self-criticism
options.description: Tell yourself you’re behind and must push until late night, even if anxious increases.

ei.rationale: Elevates threat-based emotion, often narrowing attention and impairing flexible thinking under pressure.

options.label: Ignore feelings and wing it
options.description: Decide emotions are irrelevant and skip preparation; assume you’ll manage tomorrow.

ei.rationale: Fails to use emotion as information; reduces adaptive planning and preparedness.
